info: Accepted: dispose in the household paper recycling bin
- Paper egg cartons, paper drink trays, clean pizza boxes
- Paper bags with plastic layers (flour bags, pet food bags, etc.)
- Paper towel and toilet paper cores
- Hard cover and soft cover books
Accepted: dispose in the household paper recycling bin
- Paper egg cartons, paper drink trays, clean pizza boxes
- Paper bags with plastic layers (flour bags, pet food bags, etc.)
- Paper towel and toilet paper cores
- Hard cover and soft cover books
Accepted: dispose in the blue recycling bin
- Paper or plastic takeout cups, bowls, and lids
- Plastic trays and tops (fruit trays, etc.)
- Plastic bottles, jugs, jars, tubs, and trays
- Plastic packaging (toys, batteries, electronics, etc. separate from paper backing)
- Clamshell containers (baked goods, fruit, eggs, etc.)
- Plastic garden plant pots and seeding trays
- Clean all food-related containers and plastics thoroughly.
- Place plastic waste into blue recycling bins.
Accepted: dispose in the blue recycling bin
- Metal cans with danger, warning, or poison labels
- Make sure all food-related containers are cleaned thoroughly.
- Make sure aerosol cans are empty before disposal.
- Place metal waste into blue recycling bins.
Accepted: dispose in the grey glass recycling bin
- Clear non-coloured non-deposit glass bottles and jars
- Make sure all food-related containers are cleaned thoroughly.
Accepted: bring to the nearest recycling depot
- Bags for wood pellets and garden products
- Overwrap on mattresses, furniture, and electronic equipment
- Plastic shipping envelopes or bubble wrap
